message:
chore(beads): close ft-5v7l0

Sync close transition for fusion-weight canonicalization in 691661d2.

Agent: RusticMeadow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-58uc0","title":"[gap] README wait-for rule-fire claim not matched by crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs","description":"README.md:144 claims `ft robot wait-for` blocks until a specific rule fires; grep shows the command is defined as "Wait for a pattern in pane output" at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:2454-2473 and implemented via `wezterm::compile_wait_matcher` + `PaneWaiter` polling at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:17782-17864.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:08.856774Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:19:01.722606Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:49:14.644794Z","close_reason":"done — README said 'blocks until a specific rule fires' but implementation waits for pattern match in pane output; fixed wording (9f9a7c40)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"]}
 {"id":"ft-5m5xc","title":"scan_pipeline: enable_ansi_analysis config field is dead — never read, setting false has zero effect","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/scan_pipeline.rs:40-62 ScanPipelineConfig declares 'pub enable_ansi_analysis: bool' with Default = true, but no code in the crate ever reads this field. process() at :405 and process_chunk() at :443 unconditionally call scan_newlines_and_ansi* regardless of the flag. An operator configuring 'enable_ansi_analysis = false' in their config expecting to disable the ANSI byte counting gets no behavior change. This is a reality-gap defect: the documented config surface lies about what it controls. Fix: gate the ansi-scan call on self.config.enable_ansi_analysis; when false, skip the scan and return a ScanMetricsSummary with ansi_byte_count=0 and ansi_density=0 (preserving the newline_count + logical_lines numbers that don't depend on ANSI scanning). Default stays true so existing callers see no change. Regression test: set enable_ansi_analysis=false, feed input containing ESC bytes, assert ansi_byte_count==0 in returned ScanOutput.metrics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:47:35.328044Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:50.053903Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:50.053447Z","close_reason":"Landed in 3a4dfcd8 (fix(scan_pipeline): honor enable_ansi_analysis flag in batch + chunked paths). Zero ansi_byte_count after SIMD scan when flag is false; scan still runs to keep state.scan_state honest across chunks. Two regression tests pin batch (process) + chunked (process_chunk) contracts, including a sanity check that the default config still counts ANSI bytes. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-5n9my","title":"storage reopen preserves synchronous FTS indexing","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:07:57.132270Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:19:19.698143Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:19:19.697874Z","close_reason":"Landed in commit 49937d56: storage_handle_reopen_preserves_synchronous_fts_indexing test pins the full StorageHandle reopen path. Asserts (1) fresh open with defer_fts_triggers=false installs all 3 output_segments_a[iud] triggers and synchronously indexes the first write, (2) reopen with defer_fts_triggers=false keeps all 3 triggers and synchronously indexes the second write via the trigger path. Combined with existing fts_deferred_mode_is_reversible_after_toggle (SQL-level true→false phase test) and fts_deferred_mode_toggle_round_trip (5-iteration stress), the ft-ih4tm bidirectional contract is now pinned at both the connection and StorageHandle layers. Added Debug+Clone on StorageConfig for test ergonomics. All 3 tests pass against /tmp/ft-rv-p0-target.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-5v7l0","title":"[golden] wa.search conformance golden freezes float fusion weights","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/tests/golden_robot_envelope/wa_search.json currently freezes raw float bytes for metrics.lexical_weight=0.30000001192092896 and metrics.semantic_weight=0.699999988079071. Those values originate from effective_search_fusion_weights in crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs:150-157 via f32 casting of quality_weight.\n\nRisk: these golden bytes are tied to representation details of the current float path rather than operator-visible wa.search behavior. A harmless implementation change (for example widening/narrowing intermediates, different serde float rendering, or moving the helper) can cause golden churn with no semantic regression. This is the same class of byte-stability issue as ft-55l4f: scheduler/config representation detail frozen as contract noise.\n\nFix direction: keep a semantic assertion on the search weights in the test harness, but canonicalize or round the serialized golden value so the golden freezes presence/meaning rather than exact float formatting artifacts.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:11:54.151063Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:24:04.848456Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-5v7l0","title":"[golden] wa.search conformance golden freezes float fusion weights","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/tests/golden_robot_envelope/wa_search.json currently freezes raw float bytes for metrics.lexical_weight=0.30000001192092896 and metrics.semantic_weight=0.699999988079071. Those values originate from effective_search_fusion_weights in crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs:150-157 via f32 casting of quality_weight.\n\nRisk: these golden bytes are tied to representation details of the current float path rather than operator-visible wa.search behavior. A harmless implementation change (for example widening/narrowing intermediates, different serde float rendering, or moving the helper) can cause golden churn with no semantic regression. This is the same class of byte-stability issue as ft-55l4f: scheduler/config representation detail frozen as contract noise.\n\nFix direction: keep a semantic assertion on the search weights in the test harness, but canonicalize or round the serialized golden value so the golden freezes presence/meaning rather than exact float formatting artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:11:54.151063Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:25:21.461737Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:25:21.461447Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 691661d2 (Option a: round to 2 decimals, matching pane 5 style precedent). canonicalize() in mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs now matches 'lexical_weight' | 'semantic_weight' if the child is a number, computes (f*100.0).round()/100.0, and stores it via serde_json::Number::from_f64 → Value::Number. The golden value is exactly 0.3 / 0.7 after round-trip because ryu emits shortest round-trip form for f64 0.3 / 0.7. wa_search.json golden pinned to the canonical 0.3 / 0.7 values. Kept the 30/70 split readable in the golden (reviewer can still see intent at a glance) instead of zeroing like score/semantic_score. Semantic assertions in assert_search_success_data at :446 already skip these fields so no test-logic change.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-5zyqe","title":"[mock] dataflow.rs:980 merged compute nodes downgraded to NodeKind::Source","description":"dataflow.rs:980 in DataflowGraph::merge() sets `kind: NodeKind::Source, // placeholder` for merged compute/debounce nodes because ComputeFn is not Clone. This silently degrades merged compute nodes to inert Source nodes — their computation logic is lost. Production behavior should be: either (a) add a NodeKind::InertCompute variant so callers can detect and re-register the compute fn, (b) make ComputeFn clonable via Arc wrapper, or (c) log a warning when a compute node is downgraded during merge. The doc comment at line 965-968 acknowledges the limitation and tells callers to re-register, but the silent downgrade is error-prone.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:32:39.280862Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:39:50.126666Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:39:50.062995Z","close_reason":"implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3599,"issue_id":"ft-5zyqe","author":"jemanuel","text":"Commit cef1c892 switches dataflow ComputeFn to Arc so merge() can preserve Compute/Debounce node kinds instead of downgrading them to Source. Updated merge docs, rewired merge() to clone node kinds, and added regression tests proving merged compute nodes still recompute and merged debounce nodes still buffer+flush. Local verification: rustfmt + git diff --check passed; UBS found no critical issues in crates/frankenterm-core/src/dataflow.rs. Requested rch run is still spending the window in full-workspace sync with no compile/test result line yet.","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:39:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-61kg4","title":"events::EventDeduplicator::check: max_capacity=0 degrades to 1-slot ghost cache (parallel to ft-bx4le)","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:843-891 EventDeduplicator::check with max_capacity=0 does NOT disable deduplication. Instead: the novel-key eviction path at line 875 fires (entries.len()=0 >= 0=true), pop_front on empty insertion_order is a no-op, and the insert at line 881-888 still succeeds. Effect on alternating keys A, B, A: each is reported 'New' (because evict-then-insert toggles which key occupies the single slot), but an immediate repeat of the same key returns Duplicate. This is the same 'zero capacity → 1-slot ghost cache' shape fixed in connector_inbound_bridge ft-bx4le (commit 8fdfd114). Fix: early-return DedupeVerdict::New at the top of check when self.max_capacity == 0, matching ft-bx4le's pattern. No state accumulation, consistent 'no dedup' semantics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:51.129305Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:52:00.572881Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:52:00.572605Z","close_reason":"Landed in bf854b26 (fix(events): bypass dedup when EventDeduplicator max_capacity=0). Short-circuit DedupeVerdict::New at top of check() when max_capacity == 0, mirroring ft-bx4le's pattern for SignalDeduplicator. Regression test dedup_zero_capacity_bypasses_dedup_ft_61kg4 pins the behavior. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-6269x","title":"[reality-gap] ft snapshot restore Unix-only status is not disclosed in README session-persistence matrix","description":"# Claim (README)

README.md:128 Session Persistence matrix row:

    | Session persistence | Supported with backend prerequisite |
    Snapshots, session inspection, restore, and `ft session doctor`
    are implemented, but live restore still depends on the current
    WezTerm mux interop boundary. \`ft restart\` is currently
    Unix-only. |

Disclosed: `ft restart` Unix-only.
Undisclosed: `ft snapshot restore` has the *same* cfg gate.

# Reality (code)

`crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:27100` (ft restart — disclosed):

    eprintln!("ft restart is currently supported only on Unix platforms.");

`crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:39715` (ft snapshot restore — NOT disclosed):

    eprintln!("ft snapshot restore is currently supported only on Unix platforms.");

Same gate, same failure mode. README line 128 specifically mentions
`ft restart` as the Unix-only surface but omits `ft snapshot restore`,
which is also mentioned in the *same* matrix row ("Snapshots, session
inspection, **restore**, and `ft session doctor` are implemented").

A Windows operator reading the matrix is told "restore is
implemented" (true, on Unix) without the cross-platform caveat.

# Failure shape

Operator on Windows runs `ft snapshot restore ...` → eprintln hits
→ exit 1. Workaround unknown.

# Recommended disposition

Update README.md:128 to say:

    Snapshots and session inspection are implemented cross-platform;
    `ft restart` and `ft snapshot restore` are currently Unix-only.

One-line doc change. Alternative: fix the cfg gate. The session
persistence code under `#[cfg(unix)]` likely depends on real Unix
signals / fork semantics — porting is non-trivial. Docs-first is
the pragmatic path.

# Related

ft-2guux previously flagged G2 "`ft restart` Unix-only not in
support matrix" and was closed 2026-04-21 with a README.md:128
update. That update added disclosure for `ft restart` but missed
`ft snapshot restore` — this bead is the follow-up.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"pane8","owner":"pane3-readme-fix","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:44:55.113196Z","created_by":"reality-check","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:18:55.619147Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:49:57.208098Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","platform","reality-gap"]}
@@ -264,6 +264,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-qyw1z","title":"[review][CRITICAL] mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs WIP uses std::env::set_var, violates forbid(unsafe_code) workspace lint","description":"Uncommitted WIP expansion of crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs (pane 2, ft-4o5mb success-path harness) calls std::env::set_var and std::env::remove_var 6 times across FakeWezterm::install and EnvGuard::Drop.

Rust 2024 edition made std::env::set_var/remove_var unsafe fn. The workspace declares [workspace.lints.rust] unsafe_code = "forbid" (Cargo.toml), so this test WILL NOT COMPILE once committed — the harness currently builds only because the uncommitted diff has not been fed through cargo check --all-targets.

Evidence:
- git diff crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs: 6 set_var/remove_var call sites in FakeWezterm::install() and impl Drop for EnvGuard.
- Cargo.toml workspace lints: unsafe_code = "forbid".
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/lib.rs:44: #![forbid(unsafe_code)] (reinforced at crate level).
- MEMORY.md already flags this constraint: 'std::env::set_var is unsafe in Rust 2024 edition — use Command::env() instead'.

Fix options (pick one, no new beads needed):
1. Drop the FakeWezterm env-var approach. Instead, pass FT_WEZTERM_CLI/FT_FAKE_WEZTERM_STATE_DIR via a builder API that threads explicit config into the MCP server (no process-global mutation).
2. Spawn the MCP server in a subprocess with Command::env("FT_WEZTERM_CLI", ...) and talk to it over stdio — Command::env is safe and the workspace policy already endorses this pattern.
3. Convert the integration test to a #[test] that uses a hand-rolled test harness struct carrying the CLI path + state dir through Config, not env vars.

Tag: review. Severity: CRITICAL — blocks merge. Discovered by reviewer pane during 2026-04-23 audit round 2.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:01:22.783313Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:32:56.460683Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:32:56.460376Z","close_reason":"Replaced unsafe test env mutation with a process-local wezterm CLI override and updated MCP core-tool conformance coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-qzunb","title":"[HIGH] mux layout_arrangement + swap_layout JSON proptests fail on f64 ratio precision drift","description":"layout_arrangement_json_roundtrip and swap_layout_json_roundtrip in frankenterm/mux/tests/proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs currently fail at HEAD. arb_layout_arrangement generates LayoutArrangement::Split { ratio: 0.0f64..=1.0f64, ... } with recursive depth. When that ratio is serialized via serde_json and parsed back, some unrounded f64 values lose precision in the last decimal place (e.g. 0.10814827875804041 → 0.1081482787580404 and 0.9177432348398312 → 0.9177432348398313), so the roundtrip prop_assert_eq!(back, value) fails.

This is a blocker: the mux crate's cargo test currently shows 2/9 failing.

Fix direction: restrict the ratio generator to values that are exactly representable in IEEE 754 f64 with decimal notation (e.g. rounded to 1/2^16 fractions, or integer tenths/hundredths). Preferred: generate as integer 0..=10_000 and divide by 10_000.0 so every ratio is of the form n/10_000 and survives JSON's decimal representation. Keep the recursive depth/size parameters as-is.

Repro:
  CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ \
    CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-pane6-target \
    cargo test --manifest-path frankenterm/mux/Cargo.toml --test proptest_tab_wire_serde

Observed at HEAD (04dc8d76).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T17:59:44.814922Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:01:15.542629Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:01:15.542244Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 10309c8e: constrain arb_layout_arrangement's ratio to (0..=10_000).map(|n| n as f64 / 10_000) so every generated ratio is of the form n/10_000 and survives a serde_json decimal roundtrip bit-for-bit. All 10 tests in frankenterm/mux/tests/proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs now pass (was 7 pass / 2 fail). Granularity 0.0001 is more than adequate for split layout wire data.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["codec","mux","proptest","regression","serde"]}
 {"id":"ft-r1hia","title":"policy tests ignore RateLimiter::check must_use seed results","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:49:20.277414Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:00:01.099468Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:00:01.099217Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 81ba31cd. Cleanup only: touched policy tests now explicitly discard RateLimiter::check seed results; behavior already covered by the no-default-features GC proof lane.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-r3mmz","title":"[review][HIGH] ft-4o5mb.1 wa.state harness expects tool-level FT-MCP-0001 but framework rejects at schema — test will fail when run","description":"commit 4e270e95 added crates/frankenterm-core/tests/wa_state_mcp_conformance.rs. Commit subject explicitly states 'verification partial - rch lane timed out after 45min'. Audit by inspection reveals a logic error that will make the test fail on first actual run.

## The bug

Test at :395-401 sends invalid args:

    json!({
        "agent": 7,
        "format": "json"
    })

Test's assertion at :274-288 expects tool-level FT-MCP-0001:

    fn assert_invalid_args_response(response: &Value) {
        assert_eq!(response["kind"], "tool_envelope");  // LINE 275
        ...
        assert_invalid_args_envelope_shape(&payload);      // asserts error_code == 'FT-MCP-0001'
    }

But fastmcp-server/src/router.rs:837 calls schema::validate() BEFORE dispatching to the tool handler, and fastmcp-protocol/src/schema.rs:211 emits 'expected type string, got integer' for type mismatches. For wa.state with {"agent": 7}, the framework will reject at schema validation with McpError::invalid_params('Input validation failed: root.agent: expected type string, got integer').

parse_invalid_args_response at :209-224 maps framework errors to {"kind": "framework_error", ...}, so response["kind"] becomes "framework_error" — the assert_eq! at :275 FAILS, test panics.

## Confirmed via

1. /dp/fastmcp_rust/crates/fastmcp-server/src/router.rs:833-849 — schema validation runs BEFORE tool dispatch.
2. /dp/fastmcp_rust/crates/fastmcp-protocol/src/schema.rs:211 — type-mismatch path emits 'expected type X, got Y'.
3. ecae6caa precedent: the mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs harness MIGRATED AWAY from type-mismatch invalid-args payloads (old: 'query': 42; new: 'limit': 0). The migration happened specifically because type-mismatch now hits framework rejection. The 4e270e95 author pattern-matched the OLD shape of ecae6caa, not the current version.
4. For wa.state specifically: all 3 schema fields (domain, agent, pane_id) are tightly typed. additionalProperties:false closes the unknown-key slip. There is NO payload that passes framework validation but fails tool-level serde for this tool — the MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS branch at mcp_tools.rs:912-923 is DEAD CODE for wa.state (related to wa-nu4.3.9.19).

## Concrete fix

Replace assert_invalid_args_response with a framework-level check (mirroring ecae6caa's assert_framework_invalid_params_response):

    fn assert_framework_invalid_args_response(response: &Value) {
        assert_eq!(response["kind"], "framework_error");
        assert_eq!(response["code"], "InvalidParams");
        assert!(response["message"].as_str().is_some_and(|m| m.contains("root.agent") && m.contains("expected type")));
        assert!(response["data"].is_null());
    }

Keep the invalid_args_response payload as {"agent": 7, ...} — this does trigger framework rejection, which is what we test. No golden regeneration needed if assert_matches_golden is still called (the golden content changes but ToolGoldenCapture structure stays the same).

Alternatively use boundary-violation payload {"pane_id": -1} (violates minimum:0) — cleaner intent-reveal.

## Severity

HIGH because:
- Test fails deterministically on first real run (not a flake).
- Blocks the test suite for any agent running wa_state_mcp_conformance.
- Lands during release readiness epic — a broken conformance test is a ship blocker.
- Commit subject admits 'verification partial'; this is exactly the risk that caveat was flagging.

Discovered during reviewer audit round 12, 2026-04-23. Tag: review, mcp, conformance, test-logic-error.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:25:15.187550Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:25:15.187550Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-r4l82","title":"Mock-code-finder sweep of mux/term/codec: 0 production stubs, ft-yv02l #6-7 re-triage","description":"Multi-method mock-code-finder sweep of frankenterm/mux (25 files), frankenterm/term (26 files), frankenterm/codec (5 files) on 2026-04-20.

## Methods
- Keyword grep: TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX/STUB/PLACEHOLDER/MOCK/DUMMY/FAKE/TEMP
- unimplemented!() / todo!() scan
- Short-function/fallback-return scan: -> bool { true/false }, -> Option<_> { None }, empty bodies
- Hedge-phrase grep: not yet, for now, best effort, no-op

## Findings table

| # | Location | Category | Verdict |
|---|----------|----------|---------|
| 1-29 | mux/src/pane.rs:603-775 + mux/src/tab.rs:4027-5100+ | 29x unimplemented!() in FakePane test doubles | False positive - inside #[cfg(test)] modules (pane.rs:595, tab.rs:4004). Legitimate Rust pattern: test doubles only impl trait methods the tests exercise. |
| 30 | term/src/terminalstate/mod.rs:141 | // TODO: selective_erase when supported (struct-field comment) | Documentation of known terminal-emulator limitation. Not a stub - the struct works without this field. |
| 31 | term/src/terminalstate/mod.rs:1251 | // TODO: see https://vt100.net/docs/vt510-rm/DECSTR.html in Device::SoftReset | Reference comment. The SoftReset handler is fully implemented below - comment is just a spec pointer. |
| 32-33 | term/src/test/c0.rs:13,36 | // TODO: when we can set the left margin, we should test that here | Test-coverage gap. Tests still run; two cases arent covered because the test framework doesnt expose left-margin setup. |
| 34 | term/src/test/mod.rs:9 | // mod selection; FIXME: port to render layer | Historical module disable. The selection test module was moved to the render layer; this comment is a bread-crumb. |
| 35 | mux/src/ssh.rs:957 | fn process_id(&self) -> Option<u32> { None } | Correct by design - WrappedSshChild has no local PID because the child process runs on the remote SSH host. None is the honest answer. |
| 36 | mux/src/ssh.rs:962 | fn as_raw_handle(&self) -> Option<RawHandle> { None } (Windows) | Same rationale as #35. |

## Corrects prior record

The closed bead ft-yv02l (2026-04-20 'Audit placeholder/stub markers surfaced by mock-code-finder sweep') listed the mux tab.rs and pane.rs unimplemented!() clusters under 'Cat 2' and deferred them as 'out-of-scope, pane 1 (mux) territory' requiring domain-owner review. That classification was incorrect - these are test scaffolding inside #[cfg(test)] modules. There is no pane-1-owner action required.

## Outcome

- 0 production stubs
- 0 actionable TODOs (all 5 are documentation / test-coverage / historical disables)
- 0 commits required
- Memory note recorded for future sweeps

## Why this bead exists

Audit trail so future /mock-code-finder passes over the frankenterm/mux|term|codec cluster can skip the scan (its mature WezTerm-heritage code) and correct the residual noise from ft-yv02l #6-7.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T03:33:07.647071Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T03:58:39.416510Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T03:58:39.416201Z","close_reason":"Verified ft-yv02l #6-7 are #[cfg(test)] FakePane scaffolding only; no production stub to fix","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-rfpk6","title":"session_restore + snapshot_engine open_conn helpers omit foreign_keys PRAGMA — cleanup paths leak orphan rows","description":"ft-s4myu fixed StorageHandle::with_config to enable foreign_keys on every connection open. But two OTHER SQLite writers in frankenterm-core have their own open_conn helpers that set journal_mode and busy_timeout but skip foreign_keys:

  - crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_restore.rs:254 — used by delete_session, load_checkpoint_by_id, list_sessions, show_session, session_doctor, and 5+ other production paths.
  - crates/frankenterm-core/src/snapshot_engine.rs:1293 — used by cleanup_sync which does 'DELETE FROM session_checkpoints WHERE checkpoint_at < ?1' and 'DELETE FROM session_checkpoints WHERE id IN (...)'. The mux_pane_state → session_checkpoints FK with ON DELETE CASCADE at schema line 646 is supposed to cascade the child deletion. With FKs OFF, cleanup silently leaves orphan mux_pane_state rows.

Fix: add 'PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON' to both open_conn helpers. Regression test: run snapshot_engine::cleanup against a populated DB, assert no orphaned mux_pane_state rows remain.

Scope: same shape as ft-s4myu (compare session_retention.rs:287 which correctly issues the pragma).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:57:28.078383Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:59:41.453779Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:59:41.453482Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-riz42","title":"Migrate wezterm-client and frankenterm-mux-server-impl from direct smol to asupersync","description":"Both crates have direct smol::Async<T>, smol::channel, smol::future::or, and async_io::IoSafe usage that needs asupersync equivalents. Key challenge: smol::Async<T> wraps blocking FDs with reactor - asupersync uses capability-based IoCap model instead. Needs design work for the I/O wrapping layer. Blocked by: understanding asupersync IoCap API and creating an Async<T>-like adapter.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CalmCreek","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:02:11.906232Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:56:01.015173Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T03:56:01.014992Z","close_reason":"Completed direct Smol removal in wezterm-client and frankenterm-mux-server-impl; remaining debt tracked in ft-xbnl0.2.1 verification finish-line work","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2733,"issue_id":"ft-riz42","author":"jemanuel","text":"TopazHarbor research: asupersync uses IoRegistration (per-type, not generic Async<T>). TcpStream/UnixStream each embed Option<IoRegistration> directly. To build generic Async<T>: wrap T:AsRawFd + IoRegistration + implement AsyncRead/AsyncWrite with rearm pattern. Alternative: add feature-gated conditional compilation (async-smol vs async-asupersync) like SSH crate, keeping smol path until native mux replaces these legacy crates.","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:22:30Z"},{"id":2827,"issue_id":"ft-riz42","author":"OliveDesert","text":"Code-grounded execution split after validating current sources and Asupersync primitives on 2026-04-03.

What the code says now
- `frankenterm/client` and `crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl` are the direct Smol entrypoints.
- Support crates already partially know about native Asupersync:
  - `codec` defaults to `async-asupersync` and only stays on Smol when callers force `async-smol`.
  - `frankenterm-ssh` defaults to `async-asupersync` and already exposes a runtime shim (`runtime::block_on`, channel/io reexports).
  - `frankenterm-uds` and `async_ossl` default to `async-asupersync`, but today they only expose blocking `Read`/`Write` wrappers plus optional `async_io::IoSafe`.
- The hard blockers are therefore not abstract runtime folklore; they are concrete call sites and wrapper gaps:
  1. local Unix / mux-server dispatch path
  2. SSH proxy / pipe path
  3. TLS/OpenSSL path

Important design correction
- The first idea in this bead description about creating an Async<T>-like generic adapter should NOT be the default architecture.
- Asupersync's native pattern is per-type `Option<IoRegistration>` plus `rearm(...)` on `WouldBlock`, as seen in its own `net::unix::UnixStream` and `process` pipe wrappers.
- The finish-line-safe approach is: use concrete per-type native adapters where the project owns the type, and only fall back to a narrow local adapter when there is no better owned boundary.

Recommended implementation slicing
1. Local Unix + mux-server dispatch first.
   files: `frankenterm/uds/src/lib.rs`, `crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/src/dispatch.rs`, `frankenterm/client/src/client.rs`, relevant `Cargo.toml` files.
   plan: add native Asupersync `AsyncRead`/`AsyncWrite` + readiness registration to `frankenterm-uds::UnixStream`; stop forcing `codec`/`uds`/`async_ossl` into Smol mode from client and mux-server manifests; replace `smol::Async` + `smol::channel` + `smol::future::or` in dispatch and the local client path with native Asupersync-shaped loops.

2. SSH proxy / pipe path second.
   files: `frankenterm/client/src/client.rs`, possibly `frankenterm/ssh/*` only if needed.
   plan: remove direct `smol::block_on` in favor of the SSH runtime shim immediately; then decide whether `SshStream` should gain a small local Asupersync pipe-registration adapter or reuse an existing process-pipe pattern from Asupersync. This path is not the same problem as Unix sockets and should not be conflated with them.

3. TLS/OpenSSL path third, coordinated with finish-line networking work.
   files: `frankenterm/client/src/client.rs`, `frankenterm/async_ossl/src/lib.rs`.
   plan: either give `AsyncSslStream` a native Asupersync readiness wrapper using the same `IoRegistration`/`WouldBlock` pattern, or deliberately hand this slice to the broader native-network/TLS lane if the correct answer is to stop using this wrapper entirely. Do not hide TLS behind another executor-neutral shim.

Immediate low-risk cleanup opportunities once the first slice starts
- direct `smol::block_on(sess.exec(...))` in `client.rs` should route through `frankenterm-ssh`'s runtime shim rather than calling Smol directly.
- `smol::Timer::after(...)` in `client.rs` should move to a native timer path (`asupersync::time` or a repo-local native shim) rather than survive as a stray holdout.

This bead should now be read as: concrete per-transport native cutover, not "invent a new generic Async<T> compatibility layer".","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:04:26Z"},{"id":2831,"issue_id":"ft-riz42","author":"OliveDesert","text":"Progress update 2026-04-04.

Completed in-code cleanup before the larger stream-wrapper migration:
- `frankenterm/client/src/client.rs`
  - `client_thread(...)` now uses `promise::spawn::block_on(...)` instead of importing Smol `block_on`.
  - SSH exec call sites now use `wezterm_ssh::runtime::block_on(...)` instead of `smol::block_on(...)`.
  - version-compat timeout now uses `promise::spawn::sleep(...)` instead of `smol::Timer::after(...)`.
- `frankenterm/client/src/domain.rs`
  - title propagation debounce now uses `promise::spawn::sleep(...)` instead of `smol::Timer::after(...)`.

This does NOT close the bead. Remaining direct Smol/async-io debt in `client.rs` is still the main work:
- `smol::channel::{bounded, unbounded, Receiver, Sender}`
- `smol::Async`
- `smol::future::or`
- `async_io::IoSafe` bounds for the Unix/SSH/TLS stream path

Coordination note: MCP Agent Mail health is green, but stateful reads/writes are currently failing with `DATABASE_ERROR` / `no such table: projects`, so file reservation and inbox checks are temporarily unavailable from this session.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:08:29Z"},{"id":2834,"issue_id":"ft-riz42","author":"OliveDesert","text":"Implemented the first native Asupersync transport slice in frankenterm/uds/src/lib.rs: UnixStream now carries feature-gated async-asupersync AsyncRead/AsyncWrite(+vectored) impls backed by lazy nonblocking conversion plus IoRegistration re-arm, without removing the existing blocking Read/Write surface. Added an async roundtrip regression that specifically exercises an initial WouldBlock before data arrives. Remote cargo check caught a broad test ambiguity from importing AsyncReadExt/AsyncWriteExt at module scope; that is fixed by scoping the imports to the new async test, and a fresh rch check is now in flight. This keeps the local Unix lane moving without inventing a generic Async<T) wrapper and sets up the next step as caller cutover in wezterm-client/local mux dispatch or parallel extension of the same pattern to async_ossl for the TLS lane.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:24:55Z"},{"id":2839,"issue_id":"ft-riz42","author":"jemanuel","text":"2026-04-04 progress update.

Verified remotely via rch:
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p async_ossl --all-targets` passed after fixing the dual-trait vectored write ambiguity in `frankenterm/async_ossl/src/lib.rs`.
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p wezterm-client --all-targets` passed after moving `wezterm-client` Unix/TLS transport ownership onto the native wrappers: `frankenterm/client/src/client.rs` now boxes raw `UnixStream` and `AsyncSslStream` directly, and `frankenterm/client/Cargo.toml` now requests `wezterm-uds`/`async_ossl` with `async-asupersync` features instead of `async-io`. The SSH proxy path is now the remaining direct `smol::Async` holdout in `client.rs`.

`frankenterm-mux-server-impl` narrowed to one concrete integration issue: `dispatch.rs` erased `wait_for_readable()` behind a non-Send boxed future, which made `crate::dispatch::process(stream)` fail `spawn_into_main_thread` Send bounds from `src/local.rs`. I tightened the dispatch trait to require a Send boxed readiness future and kicked off a fresh `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenterm-mux-server-impl --all-targets`; result still pending as of this comment.

Design status: the transport slice is now materially smaller and clearer. Local Unix and TLS are on the owned Asupersync-backed stream types; the next native cut after this rerun is the SSH split-FD stream and then the smol channel/or loops.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:58:56Z"},{"id":2840,"issue_id":"ft-riz42","author":"jemanuel","text":"Follow-up verification: the fresh rerun `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenterm-mux-server-impl --all-targets` passed after tightening `crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/src/dispatch.rs` so `DispatchStream::wait_for_readable()` returns a Send boxed future. That clears the local Unix/TLS transport slice across `async_ossl`, `wezterm-client`, and `frankenterm-mux-server-impl`; the remaining direct Smol surface in this bead is now concentrated in the SSH proxy stream plus the smol channel/or loops.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:59:45Z"},{"id":2923,"issue_id":"ft-riz42","author":"CalmCreek","text":"Taking the next concrete slice of ft-riz42: switching wezterm-client and frankenterm-mux-server-impl onto codec async-asupersync, replacing their remaining smol channel/or coordination loops, and removing the SSH smol::Async<SshStream> wrapper in favor of a native split-FD readiness wrapper. I’m reading the filedescriptor/asupersync APIs now before patching.","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:09:27Z"},{"id":2925,"issue_id":"ft-riz42","author":"CalmCreek","text":"Implemented the remaining direct Smol cut in the current ft-riz42 slice. Changes: frankenterm/client and crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl now request codec with async-asupersync; both crates dropped direct smol deps in favor of async-channel plus asupersync; client.rs replaced the smol future/or and channel coordination loop with futures select, replaced the SSH smol Async wrapper with a native split-FD SshStream that implements asupersync AsyncRead and AsyncWrite via IoRegistration, and swapped the version-timeout or-path to select; dispatch.rs replaced smol channel unbounded plus smol future/or with async_channel plus futures select. Verification: rch exec -- cargo check -p wezterm-client -p frankenterm-mux-server-impl --all-targets passed after the borrow and lifetime fixes; broad rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets is running now. Workspace cargo fmt --check currently reports unrelated pre-existing diffs outside my edit surface, while rustfmt --check passes on the two edited Rust files.","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:36:17Z"}]}
